title: Bridge Essay: The Embodied-Enactive-Interactive Brain: Bridging Neuroscience and Creative Arts Therapies.
concept: Mind not a computer — embodied, embedded, enactive, extended cognition.

body.text: This bridge paper connects the esoteric/philosophical concept of **Mind as Computer Critique** with mechanistic science from PubMed/MEDLINE.
**Bridge rationale:** 4E cognition — embodied, embedded, enactive, extended mind; critique of computationalism.
**Phase context:** Mind not a computer — embodied, embedded, enactive, extended cognition.

**The Embodied-Enactive-Interactive Brain: Bridging Neuroscience and Creative Arts Therapies.** — Front Psychol (2021). DOI: 10.3389/fpsyg.2021.634079. PMID: 33995190. The recognition and incorporation of evidence-based neuroscientific concepts into creative arts therapeutic knowledge and practice seem valuable and advantageous for the purpose of integration and professional development. Moreover, exhilarating insights from the field of neuroscience coincide with the nature, conceptualization, goals, and methods of Creative Arts Therapies (CATs), enabling comprehensive understandings of the clinical landscape, from a translational perspective.
